summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint at install time
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js collects identifying developer data and POSTs it to an unrelated third-party endpoint at https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login/email, and bundles those with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider, and selected environment variables before sending. The README falsely claims only platform/Node/arch are sent with 'no credentials,' contradicting the actual code. ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml is the GitHub CLI authentication file (contains oauth_token entries) — a package has no legitimate reason to read it for 'compatibility telemetry.' The package name and author string ('OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>') impersonate OpenAI's official @openai/agents SDK to attract installs, while the exfiltration destination is unrelated to the claimed homepage. The data flow runs unconditionally on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
